article_meta.title: Police turned him over to ICE

reasoning: Multiple attribution violations by flagging quoted/attributed language, use of non-standard bias labels, and several missed clear instances of emotive/loaded author prose.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flagged attributed/quoted content as author bias (e.g., brother’s statement about no crash/injury; Graber’s ‘sweep up’; Allen’s ‘ensnared’; and a direct quote ‘hurled out of the country’).
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Some flagged items are defensible (emotive narration, sympathetic framing), but others are overreach or rely on attributed sources rather than author voice.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed clear author-level loaded/sensational phrases such as ‘hail of bullets,’ ‘body ripped apart by rounds,’ ‘uncorked a wave of controversy,’ ‘bristled,’ and ‘rowdy,’ as well as repeated ‘controversial’ descriptors.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Used non-listed categories (e.g., ‘Framing Bias,’ ‘Source Bias,’ ‘Emotional Language/Loaded Language’) instead of the specified taxonomy (e.g., Sensationalism/Emotionalism, Slant, Bias by Omission).
